title: 401. The Indus Valley Civilization is also known by which other name?
hint: It is named after the first site that was discovered.
summary: The Indus Valley Civilization is also known as the Harappan Civilization because Harappa was the first site discovered.
questionTags: History,Ancient History,Indus Valley Civilization,Harappan Civilization,UPSC,RPSC,SSC,Bank PO

title: 402. In which present-day country is Harappa located?
hint: It lies in Punjab province outside India.
summary: Harappa is located in present-day Pakistan.

title: 403. Who first discovered Harappa in 1921?
hint: An archaeologist working under ASI.
summary: Harappa was discovered by Daya Ram Sahni in 1921.

title: 404. Which river was closely associated with the Indus Valley Civilization?
hint: The civilization gets its name from this river.
summary: The Indus River was the principal river associated with the civilization.

title: 405. The mature phase of the Indus Valley Civilization is generally dated to which period?
hint: It falls roughly in the Bronze Age.
summary: The mature Harappan phase is generally dated between 2600 BCE and 1900 BCE.

title: 406. Which metal was NOT known to the Harappans?
hint: This metal became common much later in India.
summary: Iron was not known to the Harappans during the mature phase.

title: 407. Which animal appears most frequently on Harappan seals?
hint: It is often called the unicorn seal.
summary: The unicorn motif is the most common figure found on Harappan seals.

title: 408. Which crop is considered the earliest evidence of cotton cultivation in the world?
hint: Used extensively in Harappan textiles.
summary: The Harappans are credited with some of the earliest known cultivation of cotton.

title: 409. The Harappan civilization is primarily classified as which type of civilization?
hint: It had well-planned cities and drainage systems.
summary: The Harappan civilization was an urban Bronze Age civilization.

title: 410. Which script was used by the Harappans?
hint: It has not yet been deciphered.
summary: The Harappan script remains undeciphered despite extensive research.

title: 411. Harappa is situated on the banks of which river?
hint: A tributary of the Indus River.
summary: Harappa is located on the Ravi River.

title: 412. Which archaeologist discovered Mohenjo-daro in 1922?
hint: His initials are R.D.
summary: Mohenjo-daro was discovered by R. D. Banerji.

title: 413. Which feature is regarded as the most remarkable achievement of Harappan town planning?
hint: Every house was connected to it.
summary: The drainage system was one of the most advanced features of Harappan urban planning.

title: 414. Which material was most commonly used in Harappan buildings?
hint: The bricks were standardized in size.
summary: Baked bricks were widely used in Harappan urban construction.

title: 415. Which famous bronze sculpture was discovered at Mohenjo-daro?
hint: A young female figure.
summary: The Dancing Girl is one of the most celebrated bronze sculptures of the Harappan civilization.

title: 416. Which stone sculpture discovered at Mohenjo-daro is known for its dignified appearance?
hint: Often shown wearing a patterned shawl.
summary: The Priest-King sculpture is a famous artifact from Mohenjo-daro.

title: 417. Which animal is notably absent from Harappan seals?
hint: It became very important in later Vedic culture.
summary: The horse is generally absent or extremely rare in Harappan seals.

title: 418. Standardized weights found in Harappan sites indicate development of what?
hint: Essential for buying and selling goods.
summary: Standardized weights indicate a well-developed trade and commercial system.

title: 419. The Harappans had trade relations with which ancient civilization referred to as 'Meluhha' in Mesopotamian records?
hint: Historians identify Meluhha with a civilization from South Asia.
summary: Mesopotamian records refer to the Harappan region as Meluhha.

title: 420. Which evidence suggests the Harappans practiced long-distance trade?
hint: Many seals have been found outside the civilization's core area.
summary: Harappan seals found in Mesopotamia indicate long-distance trade connections.

title: 421. Which site is famous for the Great Bath?
hint: One of the largest cities of the civilization.
summary: The Great Bath was discovered at Mohenjo-daro.

title: 422. What was the likely purpose of the Great Bath?
hint: Most historians associate it with social or religious activity rather than recreation.
summary: The Great Bath is believed to have been used for ritual bathing.

title: 423. Which feature best reflects the advanced civic planning of the Harappans?
hint: Cities were built according to a planned layout.
summary: The grid-pattern street layout is a hallmark of Harappan urban planning.

title: 424. The absence of large palaces and royal tombs suggests what about Harappan society?
hint: Archaeologists debate the nature of political authority.
summary: The lack of monumental royal structures suggests a different political organization compared to Egypt or Mesopotamia.

title: 425. Which of the following is considered a characteristic feature of Harappan urban centers?
hint: It helped maintain hygiene throughout the city.
summary: Covered drains connected to houses are among the most distinctive features of Harappan cities.

title: 426. Which Harappan site is famous for its dockyard?
hint: Located in present-day Gujarat and associated with maritime trade.
summary: Lothal is famous for its dockyard, which indicates overseas trade activities.

title: 427. In which present-day state is Lothal located?
hint: The state has the longest coastline in India.
summary: Lothal is located in Gujarat.

title: 428. Which Harappan site is known for its sophisticated water conservation system?
hint: Located in the Rann of Kutch region.
summary: Dholavira is renowned for its advanced water management and reservoir system.

title: 429. Which Harappan site yielded evidence of a large signboard with Indus script symbols?
hint: It is one of the most important sites in Gujarat.
summary: A large signboard with Indus script symbols was discovered at Dholavira.

title: 430. Which Harappan site is famous for the earliest evidence of a ploughed field?
hint: Located in present-day Rajasthan.
summary: Kalibangan provides evidence of a ploughed agricultural field.

title: 431. Kalibangan is located in which Indian state?
hint: The state contains the Thar Desert.
summary: Kalibangan is located in Rajasthan.

title: 432. Evidence of fire altars has been found prominently at which Harappan site?
hint: This site is often linked to ritual practices.
summary: Kalibangan is famous for evidence of fire altars.

title: 433. Which Harappan site is located in Haryana and shows both pre-Harappan and Harappan phases?
hint: Frequently asked together with Kalibangan.
summary: Banawali in Haryana shows both pre-Harappan and Harappan cultural phases.

title: 434. Which Harappan site is considered the largest known Indus Valley Civilization site in India?
hint: Located in Haryana and excavated extensively in recent years.
summary: Rakhigarhi is currently considered the largest known Harappan site in India.

title: 435. Which Harappan site is famous as a bead-making center?
hint: It lacked a citadel unlike many major Harappan cities.
summary: Chanhudaro was an important center of bead manufacturing.

title: 436. Which Harappan site is associated with controversial evidence of horse remains?
hint: Located in Gujarat.
summary: Surkotada is known for remains claimed by some archaeologists to belong to horses.

title: 437. Which Harappan site is located in Punjab, India?
hint: It lies near the Sutlej River.
summary: Ropar (Rupnagar) is an important Harappan site in Punjab.

title: 438. Which site provides evidence of Harappan settlement extending into present-day Uttar Pradesh?
hint: Located in Baghpat district.
summary: Alamgirpur represents the easternmost extent of the Harappan Civilization.

title: 439. Which Harappan site is associated with massive reservoirs and a unique city layout of three divisions?
hint: A UNESCO World Heritage Site in Gujarat.
summary: Dholavira had a unique city plan consisting of citadel, middle town and lower town.

title: 440. Which Harappan site is often called the 'Manchester of the Indus Civilization' because of bead manufacturing?
hint: Same site known for specialized craft production.
summary: Chanhudaro is often called the Manchester of the Harappan Civilization.

title: 441. Which deity-like figure on a Harappan seal is sometimes referred to as Proto-Shiva?
hint: The figure is shown seated in a yogic posture.
summary: The Pashupati Seal depicts a horned figure often interpreted as Proto-Shiva.

title: 442. Numerous terracotta female figurines suggest possible worship of whom?
hint: Associated with fertility.
summary: Terracotta female figurines are often interpreted as Mother Goddess representations.

title: 443. Which theory regarding the decline of the Harappan Civilization is no longer widely accepted?
hint: It was popularized by Mortimer Wheeler.
summary: The Aryan invasion theory is no longer widely accepted as the primary cause of Harappan decline.

title: 444. Harappan weights were generally based on which numerical system?
hint: Powers and multiples of a particular number.
summary: Harappan weights followed a binary system with standardized ratios.

title: 445. Which section of a Harappan city was usually built on a raised platform?
hint: It often contained public buildings.
summary: The citadel was built on an elevated platform and housed important structures.

title: 446. Which Harappan site has yielded evidence of a cemetery known as Cemetery H?
hint: One of the two major cities of the civilization.
summary: Cemetery H was discovered at Harappa and is associated with the Late Harappan phase.

title: 447. Which site provides evidence of interaction between Harappan and later cultural phases in Punjab?
hint: Located on the Sutlej River.
summary: Ropar shows a continuous cultural sequence from Harappan to later periods.

title: 448. Which Harappan site lies closest to the Arabian Sea and played a major role in maritime trade?
hint: Known for its dockyard.
summary: Lothal was one of the most important centers of Harappan maritime trade.

title: 449. Which statement best describes the Harappan economy?
hint: It was not dependent on a single activity.
summary: The Harappan economy was based on agriculture, trade, crafts and animal husbandry.

title: 450. Which feature distinguishes the Indus Valley Civilization from contemporary Egypt and Mesopotamia?
hint: Think about what archaeologists have NOT found.
summary: Unlike Egypt and Mesopotamia, the Harappans left no clear evidence of massive royal monuments, palaces or pyramids.
